summary:
The square knot consists of a double overhand throw and then one more single overhand throw in the reverse direction. This configuration provides secure closure with minimal tension during suture placement in surgical procedures such as dental surgeries where precise control is essential for tissue handling.

Okay, I need to summarize only what Agent1 said about why they chose Option A regarding how a square knot is made—specifically focusing just on its structure based directly from their own explanation without adding external details or interpretations beyond that text provided.

Final summary must be exactly two sentences long; no extra commentary needed after this point.

Square knot comprises a double overhand throw followed by another single overhand throw in oppositional direction according to standard definitions used in oral surgery sutures. This design ensures reliable holding strength while minimizing strain when placing sutures under controlled conditions typical within dentistry practice settings involving delicate tissues.
